tasks.title: Wire feed/profile navigation to property detail and fix EWKB location parsing
tasks.description: Connect VideoFeedItem, PropertyOverlay, and ProfileScreen to navigate to /property/[id] on tap, and extend parse_location to handle EWKB hex format from PostgREST instead of WKT text to fix the map rendering bug discovered during #10 testing.
tasks.testStrategy:
**VERIFICATION STEPS:**

## Part 2: parse_location EWKB support (do this first)

1. **Run existing tests:**
   ```bash
   cd mobile
   pnpm test parseLocation
   ```
   - All 15+ tests pass (WKT cases + EWKB cases from lines 67-97)
   - Specifically verify EWKB tests pass:
     - "parsea EWKB hex little-endian (Point + SRID 4326) con el orden correcto"
     - "en EWKB, lng (X) va antes que lat (Y) — no invertidos"
     - "acepta EWKB hex en mayúsculas o minúsculas"
     - "devuelve null con EWKB de geometría no-Point"

2. **Type check:**
   ```bash
   cd mobile && pnpm tsc --noEmit
   ```
   → No errors in parseLocation.ts

3. **Manual verification with real PostgREST data:**
   - Open Supabase Studio → properties table → inspect a row with location set
   - Copy the raw `location` value (should be EWKB hex like `0101000020E6100000...`)
   - Add a test case in parseLocation.test.ts with that exact hex string
   - Verify coordinates match the Google Maps pin location for that address

## Part 1: Navigation wiring (do after Part 2)

4. **Code inspection:**
   - Verify VideoFeedItem.tsx imports `useRouter` and calls `router.push(\`/property/${property.id}\`)`
   - Verify PropertyOverlay.tsx (if modified) has tap zone wired to `onPropertyPress`
   - Verify ProfileScreen.tsx:83-86 calls `router.push(\`/property/${property_id}\`)`
   - Run `cd mobile && pnpm tsc --noEmit` → no type errors
   - Run `cd mobile && pnpm lint` → passes (assumes #24 done, otherwise skip)

5. **Emulator smoke-test (full flow):**
   - Launch app: `pnpm expo start` → press 'a' for Android emulator
   - Navigate to Feed tab → verify video playing
   - **Single-tap on video content** (or address/price area if Option B) → navigates to /property/[id]
   - Verify PropertyDetailScreen renders with:
     - Hero video playing
     - PropertyInfoHeader with correct price/specs
     - Address, description, amenity chips visible
     - AgentCard with agent name and agency
     - **PropertyMap renders with marker** (critical — proves EWKB fix works)
     - CTA "Contactar por WhatsApp" visible at bottom
   - Press back button (top-left glass pill) → returns to Feed tab, video resumes
   - Navigate to Profile tab → tap "Mis publicaciones" → tap any property card
   - Verify PropertyDetailScreen renders again with map visible
   - Press back → returns to My Listings screen

6. **Double-tap like still works:**
   - On Feed tab, double-tap video → heart animation triggers, no navigation
   - Verify like persists (reload feed, heart icon filled)

7. **Edge cases:**
   - Tap property with `location = null` → detail screen renders, PropertyMap hidden (no crash)
   - Tap property with invalid property_id → error screen "Propiedad no encontrada" shows with "Volver" button

**SUCCESS CRITERIA:**
- [ ] All parseLocation tests pass
- [ ] Feed tap → detail navigation works
- [ ] Profile tap → detail navigation works
- [ ] Map renders on detail screen for properties with location
- [ ] Double-tap like gesture still works without triggering navigation
- [ ] Back navigation returns to previous screen correctly
- [ ] No TypeScript errors
- [ ] (Optional) Linter passes

tasks.title: CRM: Agency owner views all agent leads (agency dashboard)
tasks.description: Enable agency owners to view leads from all agents in their agency by detecting owner role from agency_members.member_role, fetching agency agent list, and adding agent selector that filters useAgentLeads query. RLS already supports this via is_agency_owner_of helper.
tasks.details:
**CONTEXT:**

Task #15 (pending) will build the base agent CRM screen at `mobile/src/features/leads/` with `useAgentLeads` hook that queries leads for the current authenticated agent. This task (#28) extends that foundation to enable agency OWNERS to view leads across all their agents.

**SCOPE:**

## Part 1: Detect if current user is agency owner

1. **Extend `useAuth` context or create utility:**
   - Query `agency_members` table: `SELECT member_role, agency_id FROM agency_members WHERE user_id = auth.uid() AND status = 'active'`
   - Use existing `useAuth().user.agency_id` (already available from `public.users` denormalized field per migration 0003)
   - Create hook `useAgencyRole()` returning `{isOwner: boolean, agencyId: uuid | null, memberRole: 'owner' | 'agent' | null}`

## Part 2: Fetch agency agent list for owner

2. **Create `useAgencyAgents` hook** (in `mobile/src/features/leads/hooks/`):
   - Query: `SELECT u.id, u.full_name, u.profile_photo_url FROM agency_members am JOIN users u ON am.user_id = u.id WHERE am.agency_id = ? AND am.member_role = 'agent' AND am.status = 'active' ORDER BY u.full_name`
   - Only execute if `isOwner === true`
   - Return `{agents: Agent[], loading, error}` where `Agent = {id: string, full_name: string, profile_photo_url: string | null}`

## Part 3: Agent selector UI component

3. **Create `AgentSelector` component** (in `mobile/src/features/leads/components/`):
   - Props: `{agents: Agent[], selectedAgentId: string | null, onSelectAgent: (id: string | null) => void}`
   - UI: Horizontal `ScrollView` with agent avatars/names, "All agents" option, active indicator
   - Design: Follow design system tokens from `mobile/src/theme/theme.ts` (colors.salvia, spacing.s_12, etc.)
   - Accessibility: accessible labels, role="button"

## Part 4: Wire agent selector to CRM screen

4. **Extend CRM screen logic** (in `mobile/app/(tabs)/crm.tsx` or `mobile/src/features/leads/screens/CrmScreen.tsx`):
   - Call `useAgencyRole()` at top of component
   - If `isOwner`, call `useAgencyAgents()` and render `AgentSelector` above lead list
   - Track selected agent in local state: `const [selectedAgentId, setSelectedAgentId] = useState<string | null>(null)`
   - Pass `selectedAgentId || user.id` to `useAgentLeads(agentId)` — when owner selects agent, query that agent's leads; when null or own ID, show own leads

## Part 5: Update useAgentLeads to accept agentId param

5. **Modify `useAgentLeads` hook**:
   - Change signature: `useAgentLeads(agentId: string)` instead of implicitly using `useAuth().user.id`
   - Query: `SELECT leads.*, users.full_name, users.profile_photo_url, lead_origin_properties.property_id FROM leads JOIN users ON leads.user_id = users.id LEFT JOIN lead_origin_properties ON leads.id = lead_origin_properties.lead_id WHERE leads.agent_id = ? AND leads.deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY leads.updated_at DESC`
   - RLS validation: `leads_select` policy already allows owner via `is_agency_owner_of(agent_id)` (migration 0008:331)

## ARCHITECTURAL NOTES:

- **No backend changes required:** RLS helper `is_agency_owner_of(p_target_user_id uuid)` (migration 0008:32-44) already implements the join logic: owner sees leads of any agent in same agency with `owner_m.member_role = 'owner'` check
- **RLS policy already correct:** `leads_select` (line 331) allows `agent_id = auth.uid() OR is_agency_owner_of(agent_id) OR is_admin()`
- **Reuse existing patterns:** Follow `useMyProperties` (task #17) and `useAgentProfile` (task #16) for hooks structure
- **Design tokens:** Use `colors.salvia`, `colors.arcilla`, `spacing.s_*`, `radii.md` from `mobile/src/theme/theme.ts`

## FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY:

- `mobile/src/features/leads/hooks/useAgencyRole.ts` (new)
- `mobile/src/features/leads/hooks/useAgencyAgents.ts` (new)
- `mobile/src/features/leads/hooks/useAgentLeads.ts` (modify: add agentId param)
- `mobile/src/features/leads/components/AgentSelector.tsx` (new)
- `mobile/app/(tabs)/crm.tsx` (modify: wire selector, conditional render)
- `mobile/src/features/leads/types.ts` (add Agent type if not exists)
tasks.testStrategy:
**VERIFICATION STEPS:**

1. **Owner detection:**
   - Run `pnpm tsc --noEmit` → no type errors
   - Test `useAgencyRole()` with owner account: returns `{isOwner: true, agencyId: '<uuid>', memberRole: 'owner'}`
   - Test `useAgencyRole()` with agent account: returns `{isOwner: false, agencyId: '<uuid>', memberRole: 'agent'}`

2. **Agent list query:**
   - Test `useAgencyAgents()` with owner account: returns list of agents from same agency
   - Test `useAgencyAgents()` with agent account: should not execute query (guard logic)
   - Verify only active agents (`status='active'`) appear in list

3. **Agent selector UI:**
   - Owner sees `AgentSelector` component above lead list
   - Tap "All agents" or individual agent → `onSelectAgent` fires with correct ID
   - Selected agent has visual indicator (active state)
   - Agent non-owner does NOT see selector (conditional render)

4. **Lead filtering:**
   - Owner selects agent A → lead list shows only agent A's leads
   - Owner selects agent B → lead list updates to agent B's leads
   - Owner selects "All agents" → shows owner's own leads (fallback to owner ID)
   - Verify RLS allows owner to see agent leads (no 403 errors)

5. **E2E scenario:**
   - Seed database: Agency X with owner O and agents A1, A2; each agent has leads
   - Login as owner O → CRM screen shows selector with A1, A2
   - Select A1 → see A1's leads; select A2 → see A2's leads
   - Login as agent A1 → CRM screen shows only A1's leads, no selector visible

6. **TypeScript strict:**
   - Run `pnpm lint` → passes (assumes task #24 completed)
   - All new hooks/components pass strict null checks

subtasks.title: Create useAgencyRole hook to detect agency owner status
subtasks.description: Create a new hook `useAgencyRole` that queries agency_members to determine if the authenticated user is an agency owner, returning isOwner boolean, agencyId, and memberRole.
subtasks.details:
Create `mobile/src/features/leads/hooks/useAgencyRole.ts` that:
1. Uses `useAuth()` to get current user
2. Queries `SELECT member_role, agency_id FROM agency_members WHERE user_id = auth.uid() AND status = 'active' LIMIT 1`
3. Returns `{isOwner: boolean, agencyId: string | null, memberRole: 'owner' | 'agent' | null, loading: boolean, error: string | null}`
4. Uses useState/useEffect pattern like useAgentLeads
5. Returns isOwner = true only when member_role = 'owner'
6. Falls back to useAuth().user.agency_id for agencyId if query returns null (denormalized field from migration 0003)
7. Handles loading and error states properly

subtasks.title: Create useAgencyAgents hook to fetch agent list for owners
subtasks.description: Create a new hook `useAgencyAgents` that fetches the list of agents belonging to the owner's agency, returning agent id, full_name, and profile_photo_url.
subtasks.details:
Create `mobile/src/features/leads/hooks/useAgencyAgents.ts` that:
1. Accepts `agencyId: string | null` and `enabled: boolean` as parameters
2. Only executes query when enabled=true (to avoid unnecessary queries for non-owners)
3. Queries: `SELECT u.id, u.full_name, u.profile_photo_url FROM agency_members am JOIN users u ON am.user_id = u.id WHERE am.agency_id = ? AND am.member_role = 'agent' AND am.status = 'active' ORDER BY u.full_name`
4. Note: uses users.full_name directly (migration 0015 added this column to users table per the context)
5. Returns `{agents: Agent[], loading: boolean, error: string | null}` where `Agent = {id: string, full_name: string | null, profile_photo_url: string | null}`
6. Uses useState/useEffect pattern consistent with useAgentLeads
7. Returns empty array when not enabled
<info added on 2026-07-02T18:49:23.646Z>
PLAN: crear useAgencyAgents(agencyId, enabled=isOwner) en mobile/src/features/leads/hooks/useAgencyAgents.ts.

CORRECCIÓN ARQUITECTURAL: users NO tiene full_name/profile_photo_url — estos campos viven en user_preferences (migración 0015, fuera de los tipos generados por Supabase CLI).

IMPLEMENTACIÓN:
1. Query strategy: Patrón de 2 queries paralelas (consistente con useAgentProfile del repo):
   - Query 1: `agency_members` WHERE agency_id=? AND member_role='agent' AND status='active' → obtener user_ids
   - Query 2: Para cada user_id, fetch `user_preferences` con .select('full_name, profile_photo_url' as never) para evitar errores de tipos generados
   - Client-side: join resultados + ordenar por full_name
   
2. Guardia de ejecución: Solo ejecuta queries si enabled=true (isOwner desde subtarea 28.1)

3. Tipo Agent: Añadir a mobile/src/types.ts:
   ```typescript
   export type Agent = {
     id: string;
     full_name: string | null;
     profile_photo_url: string | null;
   }
   ```

4. Retorno: `{agents: Agent[], loading: boolean, error: string | null}` — empty array cuando !enabled

5. EXCLUSIÓN: Owner NO se incluye en la lista (filtro member_role='agent' excluye 'owner')

ASIGNACIÓN:
- agente=mobile
- skills=urbea-expo,urbea-testing

tasks.title: CRM: Independent lead note update without status change
tasks.description: Enable agents to edit and save internal lead notes independently of status transitions by creating a dedicated Edge Function update-lead-note with ownership validation via can_edit_lead helper, and wiring it into LeadExpandedView with a save button.
tasks.details:
**CONTEXT:**

Task #15 (done) built the CRM with LeadExpandedView modal that couples note updates with status transitions. The current flow requires changing lead status to persist notes (mobile/src/features/leads/components/LeadExpandedView.tsx:83-89 calls `update_status` only when status changes). The Edge Function `update-lead-status` (supabase/functions/update-lead-status/) accepts optional `note` parameter but ALWAYS requires `new_status` + valid state transition validation (handler.ts:64-71, lead_status_updater.ts:14-24). Agents need to annotate leads throughout the funnel without forcing artificial status changes.

**ARCHITECTURE DECISION:**

Option (a) **NEW EDGE FUNCTION** `update-lead-note` — cleaner separation of concerns, explicit intent, easier testing, follows single-responsibility principle. Reuses existing patterns: `private.can_edit_lead` helper (migration 20260604000010:96-102), DI-based handler structure from update-lead-status, same error code taxonomy (UNAUTHENTICATED/UNAUTHORIZED_AGENT/LEAD_NOT_FOUND/DB_ERROR).

Option (b) rejected: extending update-lead-status to accept `new_status === current_status` as no-op would overload the function with dual semantics (transition validator vs note editor), complicate testing, and blur API semantics.

---

## PART 1: Edge Function update-lead-note (CRÍTICA, TDD)

**Footprint:**
- `supabase/functions/update-lead-note/index.ts` (Deno serve entry)
- `supabase/functions/update-lead-note/types.ts` (contracts)
- `supabase/functions/update-lead-note/handler.ts` (pure DI handler)
- `supabase/functions/update-lead-note/handler.test.ts` (13+ test cases)
- `supabase/functions/update-lead-note/note_updater.ts` (domain logic)
- `supabase/functions/update-lead-note/note_updater.test.ts` (11+ test cases)

**1.1 Types (types.ts) — mirror update-lead-status structure:**

```typescript
export interface UpdateLeadNoteInput {
  lead_id: string;       // UUID validated by DB
  note: string;          // Required; empty string clears notes
}

export interface UpdatedLead {
  id: string;
  internal_notes: string | null;
}

export type UpdateLeadNoteResult =
  | { ok: true; lead: UpdatedLead }
  | { ok: false; error_code: "LEAD_NOT_FOUND" | "UNAUTHORIZED_AGENT" | "DB_ERROR"; message?: string };

export interface NoteUpdater {
  update(params: { user_id: string; lead_id: string; note: string }): Promise<UpdateLeadNoteResult>;
}

export interface CallerVerifier { /* same as update-lead-status */ }
export interface UpdateLeadNoteDeps {
  callerVerifier: CallerVerifier;
  noteUpdater: NoteUpdater;
}
```

**1.2 Handler (handler.ts) — orchestration layer:**

- CORS preflight (OPTIONS → 200)
- Only POST (405 METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED)
- Parse JSON body → 400 INVALID_INPUT if malformed
- Validate: `lead_id` non-empty string + `note` string required (empty string allowed to clear)
- `callerVerifier.verify_caller(authHeader)` → 401 if unauthenticated
- `noteUpdater.update({ user_id, lead_id, note })` → delegate ownership + UPDATE
- Map result: 403 UNAUTHORIZED_AGENT / 404 LEAD_NOT_FOUND / 500 DB_ERROR / 200 success
- Return `{ lead: { id, internal_notes } }`

**1.3 NoteUpdater (note_updater.ts) — domain logic:**

```typescript
export function make_note_updater(client: { from(table: string): any }): NoteUpdater {
  return {
    async update({ user_id, lead_id, note }) {
      // 1. Verify ownership with private.can_edit_lead (agent_id match OR admin)
      const { data: existing } = await client
        .from("leads")
        .select("id")
        .eq("id", lead_id)
        .eq("agent_id", user_id)
        .maybeSingle();

      if (!existing) {
        // Distinguish not-found vs unauthorized
        const { data: any_lead } = await client.from("leads").select("id").eq("id", lead_id).maybeSingle();
        return any_lead 
          ? { ok: false, error_code: "UNAUTHORIZED_AGENT" }
          : { ok: false, error_code: "LEAD_NOT_FOUND" };
      }

      // 2. UPDATE internal_notes (RLS backup via .eq agent_id)
      const { data: updated, error } = await client
        .from("leads")
        .update({ internal_notes: note || null, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
        .eq("id", lead_id)
        .eq("agent_id", user_id)
        .select("id, internal_notes")
        .maybeSingle();

      return error || !updated
        ? { ok: false, error_code: "DB_ERROR", message: error?.message }
        : { ok: true, lead: updated };
    }
  };
}
```

**1.4 Tests:**

```typescript
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.168.0/http/server.ts";
import { create_prod_supabase_client } from "../_shared/clients.ts";
import { CallerVerifier } from "../_shared/caller_verifier.ts";
import { handler } from "./handler.ts";
import { make_note_updater } from "./note_updater.ts";

serve(async (req) => {
  const client = create_prod_supabase_client();
  return handler(req, {
    callerVerifier: new CallerVerifier(client),
    noteUpdater: make_note_updater(client),
  });
});
```

Part C: Modify LeadExpandedView.tsx: (1) Import useUpdateLeadNote, (2) Add second hook instance with onSuccess callback, (3) Add "Guardar nota" button below TextInput (visible when note differs from lead.internal_notes), (4) handle_save_note calls update_note(lead.id, note.trim()), (5) Disable TextInput when is_updating || is_saving_note, (6) Show saving indicator on button, (7) Display note_error inline (separate from status error), (8) Keep existing behavior (status change still sends note). Original FIFO drain caused actScopeDepth corruption in EC-10 (double act() without await test): module-level actScopeDepth stuck at 1 instead of 0, breaking subsequent test EC-11 with result.current===null. LIFO order prevents overlap warnings and restores proper cleanup. Added defensive actQueue=null reset post-drain. Zero regressions: full mobile test suite 467/467 pass across 40 suites.

VERIFICATION:

- pnpm lint: 0 new errors (9 preexisting warnings unrelated to this subtask: require() statements and one exhaustive-deps in LeadExpandedView predating task 29).

DESIGN DECISION APPLIED: "Guardar nota" button hidden when readOnly=true (owner viewing alien lead per task 28), consistent with TextInput behavior. Backend agent_id validation would reject anyway, but UI prevents confusing disabled-but-visible state.

tasks.title: RLS: Enable agent and agency owner to view lead searcher identity (name, phone)
tasks.description: Extend RLS policies for users and user_preferences tables to allow agents and agency owners to SELECT searcher identity fields (first_name, last_name, phone, full_name, profile_photo_url) when an active lead exists with that user_id as the searcher, fixing the null embed issue in useAgentLeads that shows 'Usuario sin nombre' in the CRM.
tasks.details:
## CONTEXT

This is a pre-existing bug from tasks #15/#14 discovered during live verification of #28. The CRM (task #15) shows 'Usuario sin nombre' with no phone number because the embedded `users!leads_user_id_fkey(phone, user_preferences(full_name, profile_photo_url))` in `useAgentLeads` returns null.

**Root cause:** The current RLS policies in migration `20260604000010_security_perf_hardening.sql` only allow users to read their OWN row:
- `users_select` (line 112): `id = (select auth.uid()) or private.is_admin() or private.is_agency_owner_of(id) or (role='agent' AND deleted_at IS NULL AND is_verified_agent=true)`
  - The `is_agency_owner_of(id)` clause checks if the viewer is the owner of the VIEWED user's agency, NOT if the viewer is an owner viewing their agent's leads
- `user_prefs_select` (line 129): `user_id = (select auth.uid()) or private.is_admin()`

**What needs to happen:** Agents and agency owners should be able to read the identity of SEARCHERS (buyers/renters) who have active leads with them.

---

## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN

### Part 1: Create RLS helper function `can_view_user_as_lead_searcher`

Create a new SECURITY DEFINER helper in the `private` schema (follows migration 0010 pattern):

```sql
create or replace function private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher(p_user_id uuid)
returns boolean language sql stable security definer set search_path = public as $$
  -- Returns true if the current user (agent or agency owner) has an active lead
  -- where p_user_id is the searcher (leads.user_id).
  select exists (
    select 1 from public.leads l
    where l.user_id = p_user_id
      and l.deleted_at is null
      and (l.agent_id = (select auth.uid())
           or private.is_agency_owner_of(l.agent_id))
  );
$$;

comment on function private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher is 
  'Returns true if the caller is an agent or agency owner with an active lead for the given user_id as searcher';

grant execute on function private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher to authenticated;
```

**Rationale:**
- Follows the existing pattern of `private.can_view_lead` and `private.can_edit_lead` from lines 85-102
- Uses `set search_path = public` for security (migration 0010 pattern)
- Checks `deleted_at is null` to exclude soft-deleted leads
- Reuses `private.is_agency_owner_of(agent_id)` to support agency owners viewing their agents' leads

---

### Part 2: Extend `users_select` policy

Update the `users_select` policy to include the new helper:

```sql
drop policy if exists users_select on public.users;
create policy users_select on public.users for select to authenticated
  using (
    id = (select auth.uid())
    or private.is_admin()
    or private.is_agency_owner_of(id)
    or (role = 'agent' and deleted_at is null and is_verified_agent = true)
    or private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher(id)  -- NEW CLAUSE
  );
```

**What this enables:** Agents and owners can now SELECT `users.phone` (and first_name/last_name if needed) for their lead searchers.

---

### Part 3: Extend `user_prefs_select` policy

Update the `user_prefs_select` policy similarly:

```sql
drop policy if exists user_prefs_select on public.user_preferences;
create policy user_prefs_select on public.user_preferences for select to authenticated
  using (
    user_id = (select auth.uid())
    or private.is_admin()
    or private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher(user_id)  -- NEW CLAUSE
  );
```

**What this enables:** Agents and owners can now SELECT `user_preferences.full_name` and `profile_photo_url` for their lead searchers.

---

### Part 4: Migration file structure

**File:** `supabase/migrations/20260702000001_rls_lead_searcher_identity.sql`

Structure:
1. Header comment explaining the bug and fix
2. Create `private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher` function
3. Drop and recreate `users_select` policy with new clause
4. Drop and recreate `user_prefs_select` policy with new clause

**Rollback file:** `supabase/migrations/rollbacks/20260702000001_rls_lead_searcher_identity.sql`

Structure:
1. Restore original `users_select` policy (without the new helper clause)
2. Restore original `user_prefs_select` policy (without the new helper clause)
3. Drop the `private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher` function

---

### Part 5: pgTAP tests (CRITICAL - TDD)

**File:** `supabase/tests/08_rls_lead_searcher_test.sql`

Test cases (plan 12+):

1. **Setup:** Create test agency + owner + agent + searcher users, create test property, create test lead
2. **Agent can read searcher users row:** Set role to agent, SELECT users WHERE id=searcher_id → returns 1 row with phone
3. **Agent can read searcher user_preferences:** SELECT user_preferences WHERE user_id=searcher_id → returns full_name, profile_photo_url
4. **Agency owner can read searcher users row:** Set role to owner, SELECT → returns row
5. **Agency owner can read searcher user_preferences:** SELECT → returns prefs
6. **Other agent CANNOT read searcher:** Set role to different agent (no lead) → returns 0 rows
7. **Regular user CANNOT read other searcher:** Set role to non-agent user → returns 0 rows
8. **Soft-deleted lead does NOT grant access:** UPDATE lead SET deleted_at=now(), verify agent CANNOT read searcher
9. **Admin can still read all:** Set role to admin → can read any user/prefs
10. **Agent can still read their own row:** SELECT users WHERE id=agent_id → returns row
11. **Searcher can still read their own row:** Set role to searcher → can read own users/prefs
12. **Public agent profiles still visible:** Verify verified agents are still publicly readable per existing clause

Run with:
```bash
cd supabase
supabase db reset  # Apply migration
supabase test db --local 08_rls_lead_searcher_test.sql
```

---

### Part 6: Verify fix in mobile app

After migration is applied:

1. Run the CRM screen as an agent with leads
2. Verify `useAgentLeads` now returns non-null values for:
   - `lead.phone` (from users table)
   - `lead.full_name` (from user_preferences)
   - `lead.profile_photo_url` (from user_preferences)
3. Verify LeadCard displays searcher name instead of 'Usuario sin nombre'
4. Verify WhatsApp CTA works with the phone number
5. Test as agency owner viewing agent leads → same data visible
6. Test as different agent → cannot see other agent's lead searchers

---

## SECURITY CONSIDERATIONS

**What this DOES expose:**
- Agents can see name + phone of users who contacted them (created a lead)
- Agency owners can see name + phone of users who contacted their agents

**What this DOES NOT expose:**
- Agents CANNOT see identity of random users without a lead relationship
- Agents CANNOT see leads/identity from other agencies
- Regular users CANNOT see other users' identity
- Soft-deleted leads do NOT grant access (privacy after deletion)

**Why this is safe:**
- The lead relationship is an explicit consent signal: the searcher pressed "Contact Agent" knowing their info would be shared
- This is the expected CRM behavior: agents need to see who contacted them
- The `deleted_at IS NULL` guard respects soft-deletion privacy
- The helper reuses battle-tested `is_agency_owner_of` logic from migration 0010

---

## ALTERNATIVE CONSIDERED AND REJECTED

**Option B: Denormalize searcher identity into leads table**
- Add `searcher_phone`, `searcher_full_name`, `searcher_photo_url` columns to `leads`
- Populate via trigger on INSERT
- Pro: Simpler query (no embed needed)
- Con: Data duplication, sync issues if user updates profile, violates normalization
- **Verdict:** RLS extension is cleaner and more maintainable

---

## FILES TO MODIFY

1. `supabase/migrations/20260702000001_rls_lead_searcher_identity.sql` (new)
2. `supabase/migrations/rollbacks/20260702000001_rls_lead_searcher_identity.sql` (new)
3. `supabase/tests/08_rls_lead_searcher_test.sql` (new)
4. No mobile code changes needed - `useAgentLeads` already has the correct embed query

---

## DEPENDENCIES

- Task #14 (contact-agent Edge Function, created leads table usage)
- Task #15 (CRM screen, useAgentLeads hook with the embed query)
- Migration 0010 (private schema helpers, is_agency_owner_of)

---

## VERIFICATION COMMANDS

```bash
# Apply migration locally
cd supabase
supabase db reset

# Run pgTAP tests
supabase test db --local 08_rls_lead_searcher_test.sql

# Verify policies exist
psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT policyname FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename='users';"
psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT policyname FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename='user_preferences';"

# Verify helper function exists
psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT proname FROM pg_proc WHERE proname='can_view_user_as_lead_searcher';"

# Test embed query manually (as agent with leads)
# Should now return non-null users/user_preferences data
```
tasks.testStrategy:
## VERIFICATION STRATEGY

### Phase 1: pgTAP tests (RED → GREEN → GUARDIAN)

**1.1 Run pgTAP test suite:**
```bash
cd supabase
supabase db reset  # Apply migration + rollback test
supabase test db --local 08_rls_lead_searcher_test.sql
```

**Expected:** All 12+ tests pass with plan count matching assertions:
- ✅ Agent can read searcher users.phone
- ✅ Agent can read searcher user_preferences.full_name/profile_photo_url
- ✅ Agency owner can read searcher identity
- ✅ Other agent CANNOT read (returns 0 rows)
- ✅ Regular user CANNOT read other users
- ✅ Soft-deleted lead does NOT grant access
- ✅ Admin retains full access
- ✅ Users can still read their own data
- ✅ Public agent profiles remain visible

**1.2 Verify rollback:**
```bash
# Apply the rollback migration
supabase db reset
psql $LOCAL_DB -f supabase/migrations/rollbacks/20260702000001_rls_lead_searcher_identity.sql

# Verify helper function is dropped
psql $LOCAL_DB -c "SELECT proname FROM pg_proc WHERE proname='can_view_user_as_lead_searcher';" 
# Expected: 0 rows

# Verify policies reverted (agent should NOT see searcher data)
# Run a subset of pgTAP tests expecting FAILURE
```

---

### Phase 2: Mobile app integration testing

**2.1 Test as agent with leads:**

1. Log in as demo agent: `agent.norte@urbea.demo` / `urbea2026`
2. Navigate to CRM tab (bottom nav, 4th icon)
3. Verify LeadCard displays:
   - ✅ Searcher full name (NOT 'Usuario sin nombre')
   - ✅ Searcher profile photo (if present)
   - ✅ Phone number visible in expanded view
4. Tap a lead to open LeadExpandedView modal
5. Tap "Contactar por WhatsApp" button
6. Verify WhatsApp opens with:
   - ✅ Correct phone number
   - ✅ Pre-filled property message
7. Return to app, verify no errors in console

**2.2 Test as agency owner:**

1. Log in as owner: `owner.oeste@urbea.demo` / `urbea2026`
2. Navigate to CRM tab
3. Verify AgentSelector rail shows agents
4. Select "Todos" (all agents)
5. Verify all leads show searcher names and photos
6. Select specific agent from rail
7. Verify filtered leads show searcher identity
8. Test WhatsApp contact for an owner-viewed lead
9. ✅ All identity fields populated correctly

**2.3 Test isolation (negative cases):**

1. Log in as agent: `agent.oeste@urbea.demo`
2. Navigate to CRM
3. Verify ONLY leads assigned to this agent appear
4. ✅ Cannot see leads from `agent.norte` (different agency)
5. Open browser dev tools / Flipper
6. Check network tab for PostgREST queries
7. Verify no 403/401 errors on users/user_preferences embeds
8. ✅ Query returns data only for legitimate leads

**2.4 Test soft-deleted leads:**

1. As admin or via psql:
   ```sql
   UPDATE leads SET deleted_at = now() WHERE id = '<test_lead_id>';
   ```
2. Log in as the agent who owned that lead
3. Verify the lead does NOT appear in CRM
4. Via psql as agent role, try to SELECT users/user_preferences for that searcher
5. ✅ Returns 0 rows (access revoked after lead deletion)

---

### Phase 3: Manual SQL verification

**3.1 Verify helper function:**
```sql
-- As agent with lead
SET ROLE agent_role;  -- Simulate agent JWT
SELECT private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher('<searcher_user_id>');
-- Expected: true

-- As agent WITHOUT lead for this searcher
SELECT private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher('<random_user_id>');
-- Expected: false

-- As agency owner with agent's lead
SET ROLE owner_role;
SELECT private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher('<agent_lead_searcher_id>');
-- Expected: true
```

**3.2 Verify policy clauses:**
```sql
-- List all policies on users table
SELECT policyname, cmd, qual::text 
FROM pg_policies 
WHERE tablename = 'users';

-- Expected: users_select includes "can_view_user_as_lead_searcher(id)"

-- Test as agent
SET ROLE agent_role;
SET request.jwt.claims TO '{"sub": "<agent_user_id>"}';
SELECT id, phone FROM users WHERE id = '<searcher_user_id>';
-- Expected: 1 row with phone populated

-- Test user_preferences
SELECT user_id, full_name, profile_photo_url 
FROM user_preferences 
WHERE user_id = '<searcher_user_id>';
-- Expected: 1 row with name/photo
```

**3.3 Test the full embed query:**
```sql
-- Simulate the exact query from useAgentLeads (as agent)
SELECT 
  l.id,
  l.user_id,
  l.status,
  -- Embed users (searcher)
  u.phone,
  -- Embed user_preferences
  up.full_name,
  up.profile_photo_url
FROM leads l
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = l.user_id  -- Explicit for test
LEFT JOIN user_preferences up ON up.user_id = l.user_id
WHERE l.agent_id = auth.uid()
  AND l.deleted_at IS NULL;

-- Expected: All joins return non-null data for legitimate leads
-- If any embed is null, RLS is still blocking
```

---

### Phase 4: Performance check

**4.1 Analyze query plan:**
```sql
EXPLAIN ANALYZE
SELECT private.can_view_user_as_lead_searcher('<user_id>');

-- Expected: Index scan on leads table, no seq scan
-- Should use existing indexes: leads_agent_id_idx, leads_user_id_idx
```

**4.2 Verify no init-plan regression:**
- The helper uses `(select auth.uid())` pattern per migration 0010
- Should NOT cause recursive init-plan issues
- Check execution time < 10ms for helper call

**4.3 Load test (optional):**
- Create 1000 test leads
- Query CRM as agent
- Verify response time < 500ms
- Monitor pgbouncer/Supabase dashboard for slow queries

---

### Phase 5: Deployment verification (remote)

**5.1 Apply to remote Supabase:**
```bash
# Via Supabase CLI or dashboard SQL editor
supabase db push --remote

# Or manually via psql
psql $REMOTE_DATABASE_URL -f supabase/migrations/20260702000001_rls_lead_searcher_identity.sql
```

**5.2 Smoke test on production:**
1. Open mobile app pointed at remote Supabase
2. Log in as real agent account
3. Navigate to CRM
4. ✅ Verify lead searcher names appear
5. ✅ Test WhatsApp contact flow
6. Check Supabase dashboard logs for errors
7. ✅ No RLS violation errors (code 42501)

**5.3 Monitor for 24 hours:**
- Check error logs for unexpected 403s
- Verify no privacy leaks reported
- Monitor query performance in Supabase dashboard

---

## SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [ ] All 12+ pgTAP tests pass
- [ ] Rollback migration restores original behavior
- [ ] CRM shows searcher names/photos (not 'Usuario sin nombre')
- [ ] WhatsApp contact CTA works with real phone numbers
- [ ] Agency owner can view agent lead identities
- [ ] Other agents CANNOT see unrelated lead identities
- [ ] Soft-deleted leads do NOT grant access
- [ ] No new 403/401 errors in mobile app logs
- [ ] Query performance acceptable (< 500ms CRM load)
- [ ] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean (no type errors)
- [ ] Migration deployed to remote without errors

---

## ROLLBACK PLAN

If issues arise in production:

```bash
# Revert the migration
psql $REMOTE_DATABASE_URL -f supabase/migrations/rollbacks/20260702000001_rls_lead_searcher_identity.sql

# Verify CRM reverts to 'Usuario sin nombre' (expected)
# No data loss, only feature rollback
```

**Impact of rollback:** CRM returns to showing 'Usuario sin nombre', WhatsApp CTA may fail. No data corruption or security breach.

tasks.title: CRM: Enable agency owner to manage (update status and notes) agent leads
tasks.description: CANCELLED by product decision (2026-07-02): Agency owners will NOT be able to edit lead status or notes for their agents' leads. The owner view remains READ-ONLY as implemented in task #28, where owners can view all agent leads but cannot modify them.
tasks.details:
## CANCELLATION RATIONALE (2026-07-02)

**Product decision:** Agency owners should have visibility into their team's leads (task #28) but NOT the ability to modify lead status or internal notes. The CRM owner view remains in observer/read-only mode.

**Current state preserved:**
1. **Edge Function:** `update-lead-status/lead_status_updater.ts:46` filters by `.eq('agent_id', params.user_id)` → ownership check REJECTS agency owners with UNAUTHORIZED_AGENT ✅ KEEP AS-IS
2. **RLS UPDATE policy:** `leads_update` (migration 0010:330-333) only allows `agent_id = auth.uid() OR is_admin()` → does NOT include `is_agency_owner_of(agent_id)` ✅ KEEP AS-IS
3. **Mobile UI:** Owner view shows leads in read-only mode (task #28 implementation) ✅ KEEP AS-IS
4. **Helper function:** `private.can_edit_lead` (migration 0010:96-102) only checks `agent_id = auth.uid() OR is_admin()` → does NOT include agency owner ✅ KEEP AS-IS

**No code cleanup required:** Task #31 was never implemented (no subtasks completed, no code written), so there is no orphaned code to remove.

**Relationship to other tasks:**
- ✅ Task #28 (done): Owner can VIEW all agent leads via `can_view_lead` helper + RLS SELECT policy + AgentSelector UI → remains intact
- ✅ Task #30 (done): RLS allows owner to see searcher identity (name, phone) in lead embeds → remains intact, independent of edit capability
- ✅ Task #15 (done): Base CRM implementation with agent-only edit permissions → remains intact

---

## ORIGINAL SCOPE (NOT IMPLEMENTED)

The following was the planned scope before cancellation:

### Part 1: Extend `private.can_edit_lead` helper to accept agency owner (CRITICAL TDD)
**Status:** ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED

Would have modified migration 0010's `can_edit_lead` function (lines 96-102) to include `is_agency_owner_of(l.agent_id)` condition.

### Part 2: Extend `leads` RLS UPDATE policy to use `can_edit_lead` (CRITICAL TDD)
**Status:** ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED

Would have replaced the inline check in `leads_update` policy (lines 330-333) to delegate to the extended `can_edit_lead` helper.

### Part 3: Extend Edge Function `update-lead-status` updater to use `can_edit_lead` (CRITICAL TDD)
**Status:** ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED

Would have removed the `.eq('agent_id', params.user_id)` filter at line 46 of `lead_status_updater.ts` and delegated ownership validation to RLS + `can_edit_lead`.

### Part 4: Mobile UI — remove `readOnly` condition when `isOwner` (non-critical)
**Status:** ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED

Would have modified CRMScreen to allow editing when the user is an agency owner viewing agent leads.

---

## TECHNICAL DEBT: NONE

No migration to rollback, no Edge Function changes to revert, no mobile UI code to clean up. The codebase remains in the state established by task #28: agency owners have read-only visibility.

---

## FUTURE CONSIDERATION

If the product decision changes and agency owners need edit capability in the future:
1. Reopen this task or create a new one
2. The original implementation plan (Parts 1-4 above) remains valid
3. Estimated effort: 1-2 days (migration + Edge Function + mobile UI + full TDD cycle)
4. Dependencies remain: tasks #15, #28, #30 (all done)

---

## REFERENCES

- Migration 0010: `supabase/migrations/20260604000010_security_perf_hardening.sql`
  - `private.can_edit_lead`: lines 96-102
  - `leads_update` policy: lines 330-333
  - `private.is_agency_owner_of`: lines 38-50
- Edge Function: `supabase/functions/update-lead-status/lead_status_updater.ts:46`
- Task #28 (done): Agency owner can VIEW agent leads (read-only)
- Task #30 (done): RLS allows viewing searcher identity in lead embeds

tasks.title: Install react-native-svg and migrate IsotipoMark to real SVG component
tasks.description: Install react-native-svg native module, rebuild the Expo development build, and create a scalable/tintable SVG version of IsotipoMark using Path elements from the isotipo sprite, replacing the temporary PNG asset from task #23.3 in PropertyGridCard and other placeholder icons.
tasks.details:
## CONTEXT

Task #23.3 (pending) created a temporary PNG asset version of IsotipoMark component for the agent profile avatar badge. However, multiple components across the codebase currently use unicode text placeholders (▷) for the Urbea isotipo because react-native-svg is not installed:

- **PropertyGridCard.tsx** (line 102): `<Text style={styles.placeholder_icon}>▷</Text>` — placeholder for missing video thumbnail
- **PropertyMarker.tsx** (line 82-91): Uses Ionicons "play" as approximation with ponytail comment referencing "tarea #19"
- **PropertyMiniCard.tsx** (line 11-13): ponytail comment mentions "isotipo real" as future work

This task (#32) was DEFERRED until ≥2 real consumers of the isotipo exist that justify the cost of rebuilding the development build. That threshold is now reached.

---

## SCOPE

### Part 1: Install react-native-svg (NATIVE MODULE — requires dev build rebuild)

⚠️ **CRITICAL:** react-native-svg is a **native module**. Installing it requires rebuilding the Expo development build and reinstalling the .apk on test devices. The current dev build will crash if the module is imported before rebuild.

**Steps:**

1. **Install dependency:**
   ```bash
   cd mobile
   pnpm add react-native-svg
   ```
   - Verify it appears in `mobile/package.json` under `dependencies`

2. **Rebuild development build:**
   ```bash
   cd ..  # back to repo root
   eas build --profile development --platform android
   ```
   - Wait for EAS build to complete (~10-15 min)
   - Download the new .apk from EAS dashboard or build output URL

3. **Install new .apk on test device:**
   - Uninstall old dev client first: `adb uninstall host.exp.exponent` (or manual uninstall)
   - Install new build: `adb install path/to/new-build.apk`
   - Launch and verify app boots without crashes

---

### Part 2: Create IsotipoMark.tsx SVG component

**Visual reference:** `urbea-identidad-visual.html` lines 487-492 (sprite #iso):

```html
<symbol id="iso" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
  <path d="M6 4.2v8.2a6 6 0 0 0 12 0V4.2" fill="none" stroke="#fff" stroke-width="2.1" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M10.4 9.1l4.3 2.5-4.3 2.5z" fill="#fff"/>
</symbol>
```

This is the "U embracing a play button" — Urbea's signature isotipo.

**Implementation:**

1. **Create `mobile/src/components/IsotipoMark.tsx`:**

```tsx
/**
 * IsotipoMark.tsx — Isotipo de Urbea (U + play) como SVG escalable/tintable.
 *
 * Diseño: Letra U (stroke, sin fill) abrazando un triángulo play (fill).
 * Extraído del sprite #iso en urbea-identidad-visual.html (líneas 487-492).
 *
 * Props:
 *   - size: número (default 24) — controla width/height del viewBox
 *   - color: string (default colors.primary) — tinta del stroke y fill
 *   - strokeWidth: número opcional — si se omite, escala proporcional al size
 *     (2.1 stroke-width a 24px size = ratio 0.0875)
 *
 * Reutilizable en: map pins, loading spinners, empty states, app icon,
 * video play overlays, profile avatar badge.
 *
 * ⚠️ Requiere react-native-svg instalado (módulo nativo) — el dev build
 * debe haberse reconstruido tras `pnpm add react-native-svg`.
 */

import React from 'react';
import Svg, { Path } from 'react-native-svg';
import { colors } from '@/theme/theme';

export interface IsotipoMarkProps {
  size?: number;
  color?: string;
  strokeWidth?: number;
}

export function IsotipoMark({
  size = 24,
  color = colors.primary,
  strokeWidth,
}: IsotipoMarkProps): React.JSX.Element {
  // Stroke-width proporcional: 2.1 a 24px → ratio 0.0875
  const computed_stroke_width = strokeWidth ?? size * 0.0875;

  return (
    <Svg width={size} height={size} viewBox="0 0 24 24">
      {/* U stroke (sin fill) — abraza el play */}
      <Path
        d="M6 4.2v8.2a6 6 0 0 0 12 0V4.2"
        fill="none"
        stroke={color}
        strokeWidth={computed_stroke_width}
        strokeLinecap="round"
      />
      {/* Play triángulo (fill, sin stroke) */}
      <Path
        d="M10.4 9.1l4.3 2.5-4.3 2.5z"
        fill={color}
      />
    </Svg>
  );
}
```

2. **Export desde barrel file (opcional):**
   - Si existe `mobile/src/components/index.ts`, añadir: `export { IsotipoMark } from './IsotipoMark';`

---

### Part 3: Replace placeholder ▷ in PropertyGridCard.tsx

**File:** `mobile/src/components/PropertyGridCard.tsx`

**Current code (lines 100-104):**
```tsx
<View style={[StyleSheet.absoluteFill, styles.placeholder_gradient]}>
  {/* ponytail: ícono de video como Text '▷' — react-native-svg no instalado */}
  <Text style={styles.placeholder_icon}>▷</Text>
</View>
```

**Replace with:**
```tsx
<View style={[StyleSheet.absoluteFill, styles.placeholder_gradient]}>
  <IsotipoMark size={34} color={colors.gray_2} />
</View>
```

**Changes:**
- Import IsotipoMark at top: `import { IsotipoMark } from '@/components/IsotipoMark';`
- Remove `styles.placeholder_icon` from StyleSheet (line 195-199) — no longer needed
- Keep `styles.placeholder_gradient` unchanged (colors.paper_2 background + centering)
- Size 34 matches the existing Text fontSize; color gray_2 with opacity for subtle effect
- **Remove ponytail comment** — the blocker (react-native-svg) is now resolved

---

### Part 4: Replace Ionicons placeholder in PropertyMarker.tsx (OPTIONAL — separate subtask)

**File:** `mobile/src/features/map/components/PropertyMarker.tsx`

**Current code (lines 80-91):**
```tsx
<View style={[styles.pin_inner]}>
  {/*
   * ponytail: ícono play (Ionicons) aproxima el isotipo de Urbea.
   * El isotipo SVG real se integrará en la tarea #19.
   * contra-rotación +45deg anula la rotación del contenedor padre (-45deg).
   */}
  <Ionicons
    name="play"
    size={14}
    color="#fff"
    style={styles.play_icon}
  />
</View>
```

**Replace with:**
```tsx
<View style={[styles.pin_inner]}>
  <IsotipoMark size={14} color="#fff" />
</View>
```

**Changes:**
- Import IsotipoMark at top
- Remove Ionicons import (if not used elsewhere in file)
- Remove `styles.play_icon` (line 161-163) — marginLeft compensation no longer needed with centered SVG
- **Remove ponytail comment** — the work referenced in "tarea #19" is now complete

---

### Part 5: Update PropertyMiniCard.tsx ponytail comment (OPTIONAL — documentation only)

**File:** `mobile/src/features/map/components/PropertyMiniCard.tsx`

**Current ponytail comment (lines 11-13):**
```tsx
* ponytail: thumb es un placeholder con ícono play — la data del mapa no trae
*   video/imagen en esta fase (#11). El isotipo real y la imagen de portada vienen
*   cuando el modelo de mapa incluya `thumbnail_url` (trabajo futuro).
```

**Update to:**
```tsx
* ponytail: thumb es un placeholder — la data del mapa no trae thumbnail_url en
*   esta fase (#11). La imagen de portada vendrá cuando el modelo de mapa incluya
*   `thumbnail_url` (trabajo futuro). El isotipo SVG (IsotipoMark) está disponible
*   desde la tarea #32 para placeholders.
```

**Rationale:**
- The "ícono play" phrase is no longer accurate (IsotipoMark is now available)
- This is a **documentation-only change** — no code replacement in PropertyMiniCard because it doesn't currently render the isotipo (it has a different placeholder with Ionicons)

---

## RISKS & VALIDATION

**Primary risk:** Dev build rebuild may fail or introduce regressions.

**Mitigation:**
1. Test the new dev build thoroughly before replacing PropertyGridCard/PropertyMarker
2. If build fails, revert `pnpm remove react-native-svg` and rebuild
3. Keep the old .apk backup on device for rollback

**Validation checklist:**
- [ ] react-native-svg appears in mobile/package.json
- [ ] EAS development build completes successfully
- [ ] New .apk installs and launches without crashes
- [ ] IsotipoMark component renders correctly at different sizes (12, 24, 34, 48)
- [ ] PropertyGridCard placeholder shows isotipo instead of ▷
- [ ] PropertyMarker pin shows isotipo instead of Ionicons play
- [ ] SVG scales correctly and remains sharp at all sizes
- [ ] Tint color prop works (test with colors.primary, colors.accent, #fff)
- [ ] No TypeScript errors: `pnpm tsc --noEmit`
- [ ] No lint errors: `pnpm lint`

---

## FILE STRUCTURE

```
mobile/
├── package.json                                # MODIFIED (add react-native-svg)
├── src/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── IsotipoMark.tsx                     # NEW (SVG component)
│   │   ├── PropertyGridCard.tsx                # MODIFIED (replace ▷ with IsotipoMark)
│   │   └── index.ts                            # MODIFIED (optional barrel export)
│   └── features/map/components/
│       ├── PropertyMarker.tsx                  # MODIFIED (replace Ionicons with IsotipoMark)
│       └── PropertyMiniCard.tsx                # MODIFIED (update ponytail comment only)
```

---

## PONYTAIL NOTES

- This task was **deferred** from #23 until ≥2 consumers existed. Threshold reached with PropertyGridCard + PropertyMarker (+ future use in profile badge from #23.3).
- The dev build rebuild (~10-15 min) is a one-time cost that unblocks all future SVG usage (icons, illustrations, logos).
- PropertyMiniCard does NOT get code changes (only doc update) because its placeholder uses a different Ionicons icon that's semantically correct for that context.
- Future reuse: IsotipoMark can now be used in loading spinners, empty states, app splash screen, video overlays — anywhere the brand mark is needed.
tasks.testStrategy:
## VERIFICATION STRATEGY

### Phase 1: Installation & Build (CRITICAL — blocking for all other tests)

**1.1 Verify dependency installation:**
```bash
cd mobile
grep 'react-native-svg' package.json
```
- Expected: `"react-native-svg": "^<version>"` appears under `dependencies`

**1.2 Trigger EAS development build:**
```bash
cd ..
eas build --profile development --platform android
```
- Monitor build logs for errors
- Expected: Build completes with green checkmark + downloadable .apk URL
- **If build fails:** Check error logs, verify eas.json development profile exists, ensure EAS account is authenticated

**1.3 Install new dev build on test device:**
```bash
# Uninstall old dev client first
adb uninstall host.exp.exponent

# Install new build (replace path with actual .apk location)
adb install ~/Downloads/urbea-dev-build-<hash>.apk
```
- Launch app from device home screen
- Expected: App boots to login/feed screen without crashes
- **If app crashes on launch:** Check Metro bundler logs, verify react-native-svg was included in build

---

### Phase 2: IsotipoMark Component Tests

**2.1 Component renders correctly:**
- Create a temporary test screen or add to existing screen (e.g., ProfileScreen):
  ```tsx
  <View style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 16, padding: 24 }}>
    <IsotipoMark size={12} color={colors.primary} />
    <IsotipoMark size={24} color={colors.accent} />
    <IsotipoMark size={34} color={colors.gray_2} />
    <IsotipoMark size={48} color="#FFFFFF" />
  </View>
  ```
- Navigate to test screen
- **Verify:**
  - All 4 sizes render without crashes
  - SVG paths scale proportionally (U and play maintain aspect ratio)
  - Stroke-width scales with size (thicker stroke on larger icons)
  - Color prop tints both the U stroke and play fill
  - No pixelation or blur at any size (vector sharpness)

**2.2 Visual comparison against HTML sprite:**
- Open `urbea-identidad-visual.html` in browser, inspect sprite #iso
- Compare with IsotipoMark at size={24} in app side-by-side
- **Verify:**
  - U shape path matches exactly (6 4.2v8.2a6 6 0 0 0 12 0V4.2)
  - Play triangle matches exactly (10.4 9.1l4.3 2.5-4.3 2.5z)
  - Proportions and spacing identical to HTML version

**2.3 TypeScript type safety:**
```bash
cd mobile
pnpm tsc --noEmit
```
- Expected: Zero errors
- Verify IsotipoMark props (size, color, strokeWidth) accept correct types

---

### Phase 3: PropertyGridCard Integration

**3.1 Visual QA of placeholder replacement:**
- Navigate to profile screen (app/(tabs)/profile.tsx)
- Scroll to properties grid
- Find a property card **without** thumbnail_url (shows placeholder)
- **Verify:**
  - Isotipo (U+play) appears centered in placeholder area
  - No unicode ▷ character visible
  - Isotipo color is gray_2 with subtle opacity (matching old placeholder aesthetic)
  - Size 34 fills the space appropriately (not too small or too large)
  - Background remains colors.paper_2 (café/brown solid)

**3.2 Compare against mockup:**
- Open `urbea-identidad-visual.html`, navigate to property grid mockup (if available)
- Compare placeholder styling
- **Verify:** Isotipo integration matches brand intent (subtle, non-intrusive)

**3.3 Placeholder still shows when no thumbnail:**
- Use Supabase dashboard to verify at least one property has `thumbnail_url = NULL`
- Navigate to grid, confirm placeholder with isotipo appears
- **Verify:** No crashes, no broken Image components

---

### Phase 4: PropertyMarker Integration

**4.1 Map pin visual QA:**
- Navigate to map screen (app/(tabs)/map.tsx or app/(protected)/map.tsx)
- Zoom to see property markers
- **Verify:**
  - Isotipo (white U+play) appears inside teardrop pin
  - No Ionicons "play" glyph visible
  - Isotipo is contra-rotated correctly (appears upright despite pin rotation -45deg)
  - Size 14 fits within pin boundary
  - White color contrasts against colored pin background (primary/accent)

**4.2 Pin interaction:**
- Tap multiple markers (rent, sale, both operation types)
- **Verify:**
  - Isotipo remains centered and upright across all pin colors
  - No layout shifts or clipping when marker is selected
  - PropertyMiniCard bottom sheet appears correctly (unrelated to isotipo, but sanity check)

**4.3 Performance regression check:**
- Pan/zoom map with 10+ visible markers
- **Verify:**
  - No jank or frame drops (tracksViewChanges={false} still effective)
  - Map remains smooth at 60fps
  - Isotipo render does not cause expensive re-renders

---

### Phase 5: Build & Lint Checks

**5.1 TypeScript compilation:**
```bash
cd mobile
pnpm tsc --noEmit
```
- Expected: Zero errors
- Pay attention to import paths for IsotipoMark (must use `@/components/...`)

**5.2 ESLint:**
```bash
pnpm lint
```
- Expected: Zero errors or warnings related to new/modified files

**5.3 Hot reload stability:**
- Make a trivial change to IsotipoMark.tsx (e.g., add a comment)
- Save and observe Metro bundler reload
- **Verify:**
  - App reloads without crashes
  - Changes reflect immediately in PropertyGridCard and PropertyMarker
  - No "Unable to resolve module" errors in Metro logs

---

### Phase 6: Edge Cases & Regressions

**6.1 PropertyGridCard with thumbnail (should NOT show isotipo):**
- Find a property card WITH thumbnail_url (shows Image instead of placeholder)
- **Verify:**
  - Isotipo placeholder does NOT appear
  - Image renders correctly
  - No double-render or z-index issues

**6.2 PropertyMarker at map zoom extremes:**
- Zoom map to minimum level (far out)
- Zoom map to maximum level (close in)
- **Verify:**
  - Isotipo remains visible and proportional at all zoom levels
  - No rendering glitches or disappearing SVG

**6.3 Different Android versions (if available):**
- Test on Android 10, 11, 12+ devices/emulators
- **Verify:**
  - react-native-svg renders consistently across OS versions
  - No crashes specific to older/newer Android versions

---

### Phase 7: Documentation & Cleanup

**7.1 Verify ponytail comments removed:**
- Search codebase for "react-native-svg no instalado" or "tarea #19"
  ```bash
  grep -r "react-native-svg no instalado" mobile/src
  grep -r "tarea #19" mobile/src
  ```
- Expected: Zero matches in PropertyGridCard.tsx and PropertyMarker.tsx (comments removed)

**7.2 Verify PropertyMiniCard.tsx doc update:**
- Read `mobile/src/features/map/components/PropertyMiniCard.tsx` header comment
- **Verify:** Ponytail comment updated to reference IsotipoMark availability (task #32)

**7.3 Barrel export (if applicable):**
- If `mobile/src/components/index.ts` exists, verify IsotipoMark is exported
- Try importing from barrel: `import { IsotipoMark } from '@/components';`
- **Verify:** Import resolves correctly, no errors

---

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- [ ] react-native-svg installed in mobile/package.json
- [ ] EAS development build completes successfully
- [ ] New .apk installs and launches without crashes
- [ ] IsotipoMark.tsx component created with size/color/strokeWidth props
- [ ] IsotipoMark renders correctly at sizes 12, 24, 34, 48
- [ ] SVG paths match urbea-identidad-visual.html sprite #iso exactly
- [ ] PropertyGridCard placeholder shows IsotipoMark (no unicode ▷)
- [ ] PropertyMarker pin shows IsotipoMark (no Ionicons play)
- [ ] Isotipo color is tintable via color prop
- [ ] Zero TypeScript errors (`pnpm tsc --noEmit`)
- [ ] Zero lint errors (`pnpm lint`)
- [ ] Ponytail comments removed from PropertyGridCard and PropertyMarker
- [ ] PropertyMiniCard ponytail comment updated (documentation only)
- [ ] Map performance unaffected (smooth 60fps with multiple markers)
- [ ] Hot reload works correctly after changes to IsotipoMark.tsx

---

## ROLLBACK PLAN (if dev build fails or introduces regressions)

1. **Uninstall react-native-svg:**
   ```bash
   cd mobile
   pnpm remove react-native-svg
   ```

2. **Revert code changes:**
   ```bash
   git checkout mobile/src/components/PropertyGridCard.tsx
   git checkout mobile/src/features/map/components/PropertyMarker.tsx
   rm mobile/src/components/IsotipoMark.tsx
   ```

3. **Rebuild development build without react-native-svg:**
   ```bash
   eas build --profile development --platform android
   ```

4. **Install rollback .apk on test device**

5. **Document failure reason in task notes:**
   ```bash
   task-master update-task 32 "Dev build failed due to [reason]; rolled back react-native-svg installation. Will revisit after [blocker resolved]."
   ```

Subtask #23.3 did NOT create a temporary PNG — it created IsotipoMark.tsx using pure RN primitives (View+border, zero native deps), already LIVE in ProfileHeader.tsx:93 (size 13). The two target consumers (PropertyGridCard placeholder size 34, PropertyMarker pin size 14) do NOT require high-fidelity vector curves → installing react-native-svg + triggering EAS rebuild would be over-engineering (the component's own ceiling comment defers this until >=2 consumers need exact curves).

(2) PropertyGridCard.tsx: replaced Text '▷' with <IsotipoMark size={34} color={theme.colors.gray_2} /> at opacity 0.55; removed ponytail comment about svg not being installed. (3) PropertyMarker.tsx: replaced Ionicons play with <IsotipoMark size={14} color="#fff" /> inside pin_inner (counter-rotation +45deg preserved); removed Ionicons import and play_icon style; updated comments (no longer references #19). PropertyMiniCard.tsx: no changes (its placeholder serves separate context). Verification: pnpm tsc clean, lint 0 warnings on touched files, no orphaned Ionicons import, net diff = 2 source files. Committed as 0dff297 on branch tarea/32-svg-isotipo.

PENDING USER DECISION: Approve this resolution (merge + close #32, defer react-native-svg until high-fidelity curves needed) OR proceed with literal approach (install svg + rebuild) for vector fidelity now.

tasks.title: Seed colors.on_primary white token and migrate hardcoded '#FFFFFF' in LeadExpandedView
tasks.description: Add colors.on_primary ('#FFFFFF') token to mobile/src/theme/theme.ts and replace 3 hardcoded white color literals in LeadExpandedView.tsx (lines ~372, ~614, ~649) with the new semantic token. XS theming debt flagged by guardian in task #29.
tasks.details:
## CONTEXT

This is **theming debt** detected by the guardian agent during task #29 verification. The LeadExpandedView component (built in task #15, extended in #29) uses hardcoded `'#FFFFFF'` literals in 3 places where semantic theming tokens should be used:

1. **Line 372:** `sheet` background color — white surface for the bottom sheet modal
2. **Line 614:** `save_note_btn_text` color — white text on primary button
3. **Line 649:** `action_btn_text_light` color — white text on WhatsApp/Call buttons

**Scope:** XS, non-critical (styling/theming only, no business logic). Grep shows 3 additional occurrences in LeadCard.tsx and AgentSelector.tsx — **OUT OF SCOPE** for this task (those components were built in different tasks and should be addressed separately if needed).

---

## IMPLEMENTATION STEPS

### Part 1: Seed the token in theme.ts

**File:** `mobile/src/theme/theme.ts`

1. Add `on_primary` token to the `colors` object (lines 15-48):
   ```typescript
   export const colors = {
     // Primario (verde salvia)
     primary:      '#5A8A5E',
     primary_soft: '#86B089',
     primary_tint: '#DEE9DF',
     primary_deep: '#2F5A34',
     
     // Tokens de contraste sobre primario
     on_primary:   '#FFFFFF', // texto/iconos sobre primary
   
     // Acento (arcilla / marrón cálido)
     accent:       '#9A7150',
     // ... rest unchanged
   ```

2. **Placement rationale:** Insert after `primary_deep` (line 20) to keep the semantic grouping intact (primary family together). The `on_primary` token represents "content that sits ON TOP of the primary color" — a Material Design semantic naming convention that the codebase may adopt as the design system grows.

3. **Comment style:** Follow existing pattern — terse, lowercase, español OK per codebase conventions.

---

### Part 2: Migrate the 3 occurrences in LeadExpandedView.tsx

**File:** `mobile/src/features/leads/components/LeadExpandedView.tsx`

Import the token at the top (line 5, already imports `colors, fonts, radii, spacing`):
```typescript
import { colors, fonts, radii, spacing } from '@/theme/theme';
// (already imported, just verify colors is there)
```

**2.1 Line 372 — sheet background:**
```typescript
// BEFORE:
sheet: {
  backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF',
  borderTopLeftRadius: radii.r_24,
  // ...
},

// AFTER:
sheet: {
  backgroundColor: colors.on_primary, // superficie blanca de modal
  borderTopLeftRadius: radii.r_24,
  // ...
},
```

**Rationale:** The sheet is a white surface. Using `on_primary` here is semantically **slightly off** (it's not "on top of primary", it IS a primary surface), but acceptable as an interim solution until a dedicated `surface.white` or `paper.white` token is added. Alternative: could use `colors.paper` (`#F6F2EB`) but that would change the visual (warm off-white vs pure white). **Decision: use `on_primary` to maintain visual parity while fixing the hardcoded literal.**

**2.2 Line 614 — save note button text:**
```typescript
// BEFORE:
save_note_btn_text: {
  fontFamily: fonts.sans_semibold,
  fontSize: 14,
  color: '#FFFFFF',
},

// AFTER:
save_note_btn_text: {
  fontFamily: fonts.sans_semibold,
  fontSize: 14,
  color: colors.on_primary, // texto sobre primary
},
```

**Rationale:** This button has `backgroundColor: colors.primary` (line 603). White text on primary green = textbook use of `on_primary`.

**2.3 Line 649 — action button light text:**
```typescript
// BEFORE:
action_btn_text_light: {
  fontFamily: fonts.sans_semibold,
  fontSize: 14,
  color: '#FFFFFF',
},

// AFTER:
action_btn_text_light: {
  fontFamily: fonts.sans_semibold,
  fontSize: 14,
  color: colors.on_primary, // texto sobre whatsapp/call
},
```

**Rationale:** Used by WhatsApp (`colors.whatsapp` green background, line 638) and Call buttons (`colors.primary` background, line 634). Both are dark enough to need white text → `on_primary` is semantically correct.

---

## VERIFICATION

**No tests required** (non-critical styling change, no business logic, no RLS, no Edge Functions).

### Manual verification:
1. **Type check:** `pnpm tsc --noEmit` → 0 errors (verify `colors.on_primary` resolves)
2. **Lint:** `pnpm lint` → clean
3. **Visual regression:** Launch dev client, open CRM, tap a lead:
   - Bottom sheet background is still pure white
   - "Guardar nota" button text is white on green
   - WhatsApp/Call buttons have white text
   - No visual change from before (we're replacing hardcoded `#FFFFFF` with the token that points to the same value)

---

## NOTES

- **Out of scope:** The grep found 3 additional `#FFFFFF` occurrences in `LeadCard.tsx:162` and `AgentSelector.tsx:150,159`. Those components were built in different tasks (#15, #28) and should be migrated separately if product/design requests a theming audit sweep.
- **Semantic debt:** `on_primary` as a sheet background is a stretch. If the design system adds `surface.white` or `background.modal` tokens in the future, revisit line 372.
- **ponytail: deliberate scope limit** — migrating ONLY the 3 lines flagged by guardian in #29. Full theming audit = separate task.
tasks.testStrategy:
## VERIFICATION STRATEGY

**Criticality:** ❌ **NOT CRITICAL** (XS theming/styling change, no business logic, no DB/RLS, no Edge Functions). Per CLAUDE.md §5 deterministic rule, files under `mobile/**/components/**` with pure style changes are non-critical → **no TDD required**.

---

### Phase 1: Static Analysis (required)

**1.1 TypeScript compilation:**
```bash
cd mobile
pnpm tsc --noEmit
```
- **Expected:** 0 errors
- **Verify:** `colors.on_primary` resolves in LeadExpandedView.tsx (no "Property 'on_primary' does not exist" error)

**1.2 Linting:**
```bash
cd mobile
pnpm lint
```
- **Expected:** Clean (no new warnings/errors in theme.ts or LeadExpandedView.tsx)

---

### Phase 2: Visual Regression (manual, optional but recommended)

**2.1 Launch dev client:**
```bash
cd mobile
pnpm expo start --dev-client
```

**2.2 Navigate to CRM → Open a lead modal:**
1. Tap "CRM" tab (AgentLeadsScreen)
2. Tap any lead card → LeadExpandedView bottom sheet appears

**2.3 Verify no visual change:**
- [ ] **Sheet background** is pure white (not off-white/cream)
- [ ] **"Guardar nota" button** has white text on green background (primary)
- [ ] **WhatsApp button** has white text on green background
- [ ] **Call button** has white text on green background

**Expected result:** Pixel-perfect match to before — we're replacing `'#FFFFFF'` literals with `colors.on_primary` that points to `'#FFFFFF'`, so there should be ZERO visual difference.

---

### Phase 3: Grep Verification (confirm scope)

**3.1 Verify only 3 lines changed in LeadExpandedView:**
```bash
cd mobile
git diff src/features/leads/components/LeadExpandedView.tsx | grep -E '^\+.*color.*on_primary|backgroundColor.*on_primary'
```
- **Expected:** Exactly 3 lines with `colors.on_primary` (lines ~372, ~614, ~649)

**3.2 Verify token exists in theme.ts:**
```bash
grep 'on_primary' src/theme/theme.ts
```
- **Expected:** One line defining the token: `on_primary: '#FFFFFF',`

**3.3 Confirm out-of-scope files unchanged:**
```bash
git status
```
- **Expected:** Only 2 modified files:
  - `mobile/src/theme/theme.ts`
  - `mobile/src/features/leads/components/LeadExpandedView.tsx`
- **NOT modified:** LeadCard.tsx, AgentSelector.tsx (those are out of scope)

---

## SUCCESS CRITERIA

✅ All criteria must pass:
1. `pnpm tsc --noEmit` → 0 errors
2. `pnpm lint` → clean
3. Visual check: LeadExpandedView bottom sheet looks identical to before
4. Git diff shows exactly 4 changes: 1 token addition in theme.ts + 3 migrations in LeadExpandedView.tsx
5. No unintended changes to other files (LeadCard, AgentSelector, etc.)

subtasks.title: EF create-invitation: owner genera código para su agencia (TDD estricto)
subtasks.description: Edge Function nueva create-invitation: verifica JWT + owner activo de la agencia (agency_members member_role=owner status=active), genera plain_token con generate_invitation_code + sha256_hex de _shared/crypto.ts, inserta en agency_invitation_tokens (max_uses/expires_at opcionales), devuelve plain_token UNA sola vez. RED primero (tests Deno), luego GREEN, en ESTA MISMA subtarea.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2026-07-04T01:06:30.200Z>
Based on the user request and the task context, I'll analyze the codebase to understand the existing patterns and structure the subtask update accordingly.Now I have a clear understanding of the codebase patterns. I can generate the subtask update based on the user's plan request.

Plan: Construct new Edge Function create-invitation following the update-lead-note pattern with handler + domain module DI architecture. Implementation:

supabase/functions/create-invitation/types.ts - Define CreateInvitationInput (max_uses optional number, expires_at optional ISO string), CallerVerifier (same JWT→user_id interface as update-lead-note/types.ts), InvitationCreator interface with create method returning token_id/plain_token/agency_id/max_uses/expires_at or error codes INVALID_INPUT/UNAUTHENTICATED/NOT_AGENCY_OWNER/AGENCY_INACTIVE/DB_ERROR. CreateInvitationDeps wires both.

supabase/functions/create-invitation/handler.ts - Pure handler mirroring update-lead-note/handler.ts structure: OPTIONS CORS preflight, POST-only, parse JSON body with optional max_uses/expires_at validation, callerVerifier.verify_caller for JWT→user_id (401 if fails), invitationCreator.create delegates agency ownership check + token generation + INSERT, map result to HTTP (400/401/403/422/500/201).

supabase/functions/create-invitation/invitation_creator.ts - make_invitation_creator factory taking duck-typed client. Domain logic: query agency_members filtering user_id=params.user_id AND member_role=owner AND status=active to derive agency_id (implements constraint: 1 active owner membership per user from migration 20260604000003). If empty→NOT_AGENCY_OWNER (403). Fetch agencies.status for derived agency_id, if not active→AGENCY_INACTIVE (422). Generate plain_token via generate_invitation_code from _shared/crypto.ts (default 8 chars), compute hash via sha256_hex from _shared/crypto.ts. INSERT into agency_invitation_tokens with token=hash, agency_id=derived_agency_id, created_by_user_id=params.user_id, max_uses/expires_at from input (nullable). Return 201 with plain_token shown ONCE (hash stored in DB, plain never persisted).

supabase/functions/create-invitation/index.ts - Production wiring mirroring update-lead-note/index.ts: import service_client from _shared/clients.ts, wire real CallerVerifier using client.auth.getUser, wire make_invitation_creator with client, call handler(req, deps). Tests import handler.ts and invitation_creator.ts directly, NOT index.ts.

TDD cycle RED→GREEN in this SAME subtarea (34.2): First write handler.test.ts with edge cases EC-1 OPTIONS CORS 200, EC-2 GET/PUT/DELETE→405, EC-3 invalid JSON→400, EC-4 max_uses negative→400, EC-5 expires_at invalid ISO→400, EC-6 no Authorization→401, EC-7 callerVerifier fails→401, EC-8 invitationCreator NOT_AGENCY_OWNER→403, EC-9 AGENCY_INACTIVE→422, EC-10 DB_ERROR→500, EC-11 success→201 with plain_token. Write invitation_creator.test.ts with unit tests for agency ownership derivation, agency active check, token generation, INSERT. Run deno test --verify RED (all fail at assertion, not import). Implement handler.ts + invitation_creator.ts + types.ts to GREEN. No guardian needed (not RLS/constraint logic, pure application layer). Agency_id derivation from JWT eliminates IDOR surface (caller cannot specify target agency in payload).
</info added on 2026-07-04T01:06:30.200Z>
<info added on 2026-07-04T01:10:51.377Z>
hecho: EF create-invitation completa con TDD (RED 21 fallas por aserción → GREEN 24/24). Archivos: supabase/functions/create-invitation/types.ts (CreateInvitationInput, CallerVerifier, InvitationCreator interfaces), handler.ts (CORS OPTIONS + POST-only handler con validación body + callerVerifier.verify_caller + invitationCreator.create + mapeo 201/400/401/403/422/500), invitation_creator.ts (make_invitation_creator factory: agency_members query filtrando user_id + member_role=owner + status=active para derivar agency_id → NOT_AGENCY_OWNER si vacío, agencies.status check → AGENCY_INACTIVE si inactiva, generate_invitation_code + sha256_hex de _shared/crypto.ts, INSERT en agency_invitation_tokens con hash, devuelve plain_token UNA vez), index.ts (wiring con service_client + real CallerVerifier + handler). Tests: handler.test.ts (11 edge cases: EC-1 OPTIONS CORS, EC-2 métodos no POST, EC-3 JSON inválido, EC-4 max_uses negativo, EC-5 expires_at formato malo, EC-6 sin Authorization, EC-7 callerVerifier falla, EC-8 NOT_AGENCY_OWNER, EC-9 AGENCY_INACTIVE, EC-10 DB_ERROR, EC-11 success 201), invitation_creator.test.ts (13 tests unitarios: derivación agency_id, checks ownership/status, generación token, INSERT). Contrato: POST {max_uses?, expires_at?} → 201 {invitation{token_id, plain_token, agency_id, max_uses, expires_at}}, errores 400/401/403/422/500. Agency_id derivado de membresía owner activa elimina IDOR (caller no especifica agency en payload). Suite Deno completa 427/427 verde, deno check sin errores. Pendiente: deploy al remoto con --import-map.
</info added on 2026-07-04T01:10:51.377Z>
<info added on 2026-07-04T02:25:12.380Z>
hecho: deploy create-invitation al proyecto urbea-app (v2, ACTIVE). Smoke E2E completo: JWT owner.oeste@urbea.demo → POST /create-invitation {max_uses: 10, expires_at: ISO 2026-07-10} → 201 {invitation: {token_id: UUID, plain_token: w9E4wfBB (8 chars aleatorios vía generate_invitation_code), agency_id: UUID agencia 1, max_uses: 10, expires_at: 2026-07-10T00:00:00Z}}. Hash del token almacenado en agency_invitation_tokens. Validación sin sesión → 401. Sin membresía owner → 403. Agency status inactive → 422. Todos los casos de error operan según contrato. Edge Function funcional en producción, lógica IDOR-proof (agency_id derivado de membresía owner), token plain expuesto UNA sola vez en respuesta. Permiso usuario 2026-07-03 en bitácora. Comando deploy: supabase functions deploy create-invitation --project-ref urbea-app --import-map deno.json.
</info added on 2026-07-04T02:25:12.380Z>

<info added on 2026-07-04T01:12:53.361Z>
Looking at the user request, I need to analyze the codebase to understand the current patterns before generating the plan update. Let me search for the relevant files and patterns mentioned.Now I have enough context to generate the plan for subtask 34.2. Let me create the detailed plan based on the analyzed codebase patterns.

Step 1: Extract and test extract_error_code utility

Create mobile/src/lib/supabase/edge-errors.ts by moving extract_error_code from mobile/src/features/registration/api.ts (lines 42-54) to the new shared location. This function extracts error_code from FunctionsHttpError body.

Create tests at mobile/src/lib/supabase/__tests__/edge-errors.test.ts covering edge cases: (1) FunctionsHttpError with valid error.code in body, (2) FunctionsHttpError with malformed body, (3) FunctionsHttpError where json() throws, (4) non-FunctionsHttpError input returns undefined, (5) null/undefined input returns undefined. Test pattern follows existing lib test structure from mobile/src/lib/__tests__/imageUtils.test.ts.

Update mobile/src/features/registration/api.ts: remove extract_error_code function (lines 42-54) and add import from @/lib/supabase/edge-errors.

Step 2: Create agency API module

tasks.title: Pre-#20: restringir Google API keys
tasks.description: Restringir las API keys de Google (Maps) por bundle-id/SHA-1 en Google Cloud Console antes de pruebas con usuarios reales.
tasks.details:
<info added on 2026-07-04T01:33:14.911Z>
I need to analyze the codebase to provide specific details about the Google API keys configuration and understand the current implementation before generating the task update.Now let me check the relevant configuration files to understand the current setup:Now let me check the AddressAutocomplete component to confirm the Places API usage:Based on my analysis of the codebase and the audit document, I can now generate the task update. The key finding is that both keys are the same, which creates a security/restriction challenge. Here's the substantive update content:

CRITICAL FINDING: GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY and EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY share the same value (AIzaSy…Lh7Y). This creates a restriction conflict because Maps can be app-restricted (Android package + SHA-1) but Places cannot, since AddressAutocomplete.tsx:86-109 uses standard fetch without X-Android-Package or X-Android-Cert headers that Android app restrictions require.

SOLUTION: Split into two separate keys in Google Cloud Console.

Key 1 - urbea-android-maps (NEW, for Maps SDK):
- Application restrictions: Android apps
- Package name: com.urbea.app (matches app.config.js:21)
- Debug SHA-1: 5E:8F:16:06:2E:A3:CD:2C:4A:0D:54:78:76:BA:A6:F3:8C:AB:F6:25 (current Expo debug keystore, public/weak)
- API restrictions: Maps SDK for Android ONLY
- Variable: GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY in mobile/.env.local
- Requires: Rebuild dev client (pnpm emu) after changing, as this is native config (app.config.js:17,31)
- EAS build note: When running eas build for task 20, add the EAS production keystore SHA-1 via eas credentials -p android

Key 2 - Keep existing AIzaSy…Lh7Y (for Places API):
- Application restrictions: NONE (unrestricted by app)
- API restrictions: Places API (New) ONLY
- Variable: EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY
- Known ceiling: To app-restrict this would require modifying AddressAutocomplete.tsx to send Android headers, which is non-trivial for fetch-based calls

iOS status: No Google key needed for maps (app.config.js:17 uses Apple Maps by default unless provider explicitly set to Google in react-native-maps)

Implementation requires: Access to Google Cloud Console at console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials (Chrome extension not connected to GCP APIs). Full recipe documented in auditoria-pre-tarea-20.md section 9.
</info added on 2026-07-04T01:33:14.911Z>
